question: Is the image a realistic photograph of an insect specimen preparation room?
Is the camera viewpoint a medium shot from above at a slight downward angle?
Does the image feature soft overhead indoor lighting?
Is there a dark wood work table centered in the frame with a light gray foam pad spread across it?
Are there exactly two open wooden specimen boxes arranged in a staggered upper-lower configuration?
Is the upper specimen box larger than the lower one, with its lid flipped toward the top of the frame?
Does the upper box contain multiple butterfly specimens neatly pinned with symmetrically spread wings?
Do the butterfly wings include colors blue, orange, black, and white?
Does at least one butterfly display a mirror-like metallic finish reflecting overhead light?
Does the lower box contain multiple beetle specimens with dark green shiny carapaces?
Are the beetle specimens arranged in two rows inside the lower box?
Is there a fine-tipped tweezer placed to the right of the specimen boxes?
Is the tweezer oriented diagonally with tips pointing toward the lower edge of the bottom specimen box?
Are there exactly seven insect pins inserted into a white foam strip below the tweezer?
Do the insect pins have silver gleaming heads and slightly varying lengths?
Are the insect pins inserted at equal intervals along the foam strip?
Is there a magnifying glass lying flat on the left side of the frame with a black-rimmed lens?
Is the magnifying glass covering the upper left portion of the foam pad?
Is there an adjustable desk lamp extending into the frame above the magnifying glass with the head angled downward?
Does the lamp create a concentrated light spot illuminating the interior of the specimen boxes?
Are there folded white cotton gloves in the lower left corner with fingertips pointing toward the right?
Is there a small glass bottle standing upright to the right of the gloves?
Does the glass bottle contain transparent liquid, have a black stopper, and no labels?
Is there a small electronic scale in the lower right corner resting on the table surface?
Is the electronic scale's screen off, displaying solid black with no readout?
Are there several blank white label papers with slightly curled corners scattered above the electronic scale?
Is there a soft small brush with a transparent handle and light brown bristles beside the label papers?
Is a row of light wood drawer cabinets visible in the upper background?
Do the drawer cabinets have round pulls and no nameplates?
Are there any physical impossibilities, such as objects floating without support?
